data.title: Asana Director Dustin Moskovitz Acquires 450,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameMoskovitz Dustin A.
RelationshipDirector, 10% Owner
TypePurchase
Amount$6,876,967
SEC FilingForm 4

Dustin A. Moskovitz, a 10% owner and director of Asana, purchased 450,000 shares of Class A Common Stock on July 29 and July 30, 2025, for a total amount of $6,876,967. The weighted average purchase price was $15.2401 on July 29 and $15.3242 on July 30, indicating multiple transactions at varying prices. Following these transactions, Moskovitz directly owns 55,080,999 shares and indirectly owns 4,147,046 shares through the Dustin A. Moskovitz Trust.

SEC Filing: Asana, Inc. [ ASAN ] - Form 4 - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Consumer Cos Down as Traders Keep Earnings Targets High — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies fell as investors held former market darlings to a high earnings benchmark.

Shake Shack slid after the burger chain posted lower-than-anticipated same-store sales growth.

Similarly, shares of Canada Goose fell even after the maker of fashionable parkas defied weakness elsewhere in the fashion business.

Snack maker Kellanova logged lower earnings in the second quarter.

Shares of cruise line Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings rallied after it reported a marked rebound in demand, following a soft patch in April when economic fears peaked.

Fellow cruise line Royal Caribbean sounded a similar note about demand earlier in the week, but gave back some gains after a torrid run-up in its shares earlier in the year.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Wingstop Is Maintained at Overweight by Barclays
Industrials Flat on Mixed Earnings — Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies were more or less flat amid mixed earnings.

Military contractor Huntington Ingalls Industries reiterated its revenue growth projections for the year in anticipation of busy shipyards, and still anticipates shipbuilding revenue between $8.9 billion and $9.1 billion.

Italian tiremaker Pirelli lowered its full-year revenue projection on foreign-exchange shifts, but reiterated its profitability expectations, saying its mitigation efforts helped to minimize a hit from U.S. tariffs.

The rise of the euro against the U.S. dollar has weighed on European exporters.

C.H. Robinson Worldwide shares surged after the freight company posted brisk growth in quarterly earnings, parting ways with rivals such as Old Dominion Freight Line, which pointed to macroeconomic weakness.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: GERDAU S.A. - CONSOLIDATED INFORMATION
data.description:
SÃO PAULO, July 31, 2025 /PRNewswire/ --

2Q25 Highlights

  • Adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) of R$2.6 billion in 2Q25, up 7% compared to 1Q25. The highlight was the performance of operations in North America, which accounted for 61% of the consolidated EBITDA for the quarter and offset the decline in results in Brazil and South America.
  • Investments (CAPEX) of R$1.6 billion in 2Q25, with 80% allocated to Brazil. The Company has already invested approximately 50% of the total planned for 2025 (R$6.0 billion).
  • Dividend distribution of R$0.12 per share, equivalent to R$239.5 million, based on 2Q25 results.
  • The Company has reached approximately 68% of the 2025 Share Buyback Program, having repurchased around 43.8 million shares of Gerdau S.A., totaling an investment of R$686.1 million in the program so far. Management emphasizes that the current share repurchase plan remains in effect.
  • During 2Q25, Gerdau carried out two issuances: bonds and debentures totaling US$650 million and R$1.4 billion, respectively, extending the Company's debt maturity profile.

Additional information

Gerdau S.A. (, B3: GGBR3, GGBR4) informs that it is filling today its 2Q25 results at the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and at the Comissão de Valores Mobiliários (CVM), which are available at Gerdau's website. To access this document, please click on: https://ri.gerdau.com/en/notices-and-results/results-center/.

The 2Q25 Valuation Guide is also available at Gerdau's website.

https://ri.gerdau.com/en/financial-information/valuation-guide/

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/gerdau-sa--consolidated-information-302519216.html

SOURCE Gerdau S.A.

data.title: Federal Court Says MOVEit Data Security Breach MDL Can Move Forward
data.description:
MDL bellwether cases are the first to address the largest data breach in 2023.

BOSTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / July 31, 2025 / Today, a Massachusetts federal court largely denied the motions to dismiss in two bellwether cases against Progress Software Corporation and other defendants in In Re: MOVEit Customer Data Security Breach Litigation- a large multidistrict litigation (MDL) involving dozens of class actions from around the country and hundreds of defendants. The massive data breach, which was discovered in May 2023 and allegedly linked to Progress Software Corp.'s file-sharing software, MOVEit Transfer, impacted more than 2,500 organizations and more than 67 million individuals worldwide.

Allegedly starting as early as 2021, a ransomware group known as Clop (aka C10p) hacked the MOVEit Transfer servers, stealing customers' sensitive data stored within. Affected entities include hospitals, banks, businesses, governments, pension funds, universities, among others. Plaintiffs in the MDL accuse Progress of failing to reasonably secure consumers' personal information.

"Today's ruling is an incredibly important and promising first step toward justice for the thousands of organizations and millions of individuals impacted by the MOVEit data breach," said Doug McNamara, a consumer protection partner at Cohen Milstein and one of five co-leads overseeing the MDL.

For each of the two bellwether cases, the court issued one orders that largely complemented each other. In MDL Order No. 22 (Progress Software), the court largely denied Progress Software's motion to dismiss, including plaintiffs' claims related to negligence, breach of contract, unjust enrichment and many of the state-related unfair business practices and breach of consumer protection claims. In MDL Order No. 23 (PBI, Delta Dental, Maximus, Welltok), the court largely denied bellwether defendants PBI, Delta Dental, Maximus, and Welltok's motions to dismiss largely along the same lines as Progress Software.

The five-member court-appointed leadership team includes Doug McNamara of Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll PLLC; Charlie Schaffer of Levin Sedran & Berman LLP; Karen Reibel of Lockridge Grindal Nauen PLLP; Gary Lynch of Lynch Carpenter, LLP; E. Michelle Drake of Berger Montague; and Kristen Johnson of Hagens Berman Sobol Shapiro LLP.

###

About Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll PLLC

Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll PLLC, a premier U.S. plaintiffs' law firm, with over 100 attorneys across eight offices, champions the causes of real people - workers, consumers, small business owners, investors, and whistleblowers - working to deliver corporate reforms and fair markets for the common good. For more information visit www.cohenmilstein.com.

Press Contact: cohenmilstein@berlinrosen.com

SOURCE: Cohen Milstein Sellers & Toll PLLC

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Pampa Metals Announces Name Change to Andina Copper
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

July 31, 2025 – TheNewswire - Vancouver, British Columbia - Pampa Metals Corporation (“Pampa Metals” or the “Company”) (CSE: PM / FSE:FIR / OTCQB: PMMCF) is pleased to announce that it proposes to changeits name to Andina Copper Corporation.

The change of name reflects the company’s evolutioninto a leading South American focused copper explorer holding twosignificant discoveries and a compelling undrilled copper-gold targetalong the world’s premier copper producing belt.

Joseph van den Elsen, Pampa Metals President and CEOcommented: “The proposedname change to Andina Copper represents the start of a new chapter in the history of our Companyas we execute a focusedstrategy to create substantial shareholder value through:

  • Recommencing drilling at theCobrasco copper-moly porphyry discovery in Chocó, Colombia. 

  • Rapid exploration and advancement ofthe Piuquenes copper-gold porphyry project in San Juan,Argentina. 

  • Initial assessment of the Mantaucopper-gold prospect in Antofagasta, Chile. 

Interested parties can subscribe to our mailing listand follow our social media channels.

Mailing List: https://www.pampametals.com/sign-up.html

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/pampa-metals/?viewAsMember=true

X:  https://x.com/PampaMetals

The Canadian Securities Exchange will publish abulletin announcing the effective date of the change in the Company'sname and it is anticipated that the common shares of the Company willcommence trading under its new name of Andina Copper Corporation on orabout August 5, 2025. The Company's trading symbol will change toANDC. The CUSIP and ISIN numbers will change to 03421C108 andCA03421C1086 respectively.

No action is required to be taken by shareholders withrespect to the name change. Outstanding share and warrant certificatesare not affected by the name change and do not need to beexchanged.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD |       Joseph van den Elsen | President &CEO | joseph@pampametals.com      

INVESTOR CONTACT | Jordan Webster | jordan@pampametals.com

Neither the CSE nor the InvestmentIndustry Regulatory Organization of Canada accepts responsibility forthe adequacy or accuracy of this release.

ABOUT PAMPA METALS

Pampa Metals is a copper-gold exploration company
listed on the Canadian Stock Exchange , Frankfurt (FSE: FIR),
and OTC (OTCQB: PMMCF) exchanges, rapidly advancing two high potential
copper discoveries in the prolific Andean porphyry belts of Argentina,
Colombia. The Company has been actively drilling multiple porphyry
copper-gold discoveries on the Piuquenes Copper-Gold Project in San
Juan Province, Argentina under an Option and Joint Venture Agreement
(earning an 80% interest) signed in November 2023.

In July 2025, Pampa Metals completed the acquisition ofRugby Resources (TSXV: RUG) and its Cobrasco and Mantau copperprojects in Colombia and Chile respectively. Cobrasco is a new copperdiscovery of potential scale in the early stages of exploration.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENT

This news release contains certainstatements that may be deemed "forward statements". Allstatements in this release, other than statements of historical fact,that address events or that Pampa Metals expects to occur, areforward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are statementsthat are not historical facts and are generally, but not always,identified by the "expects" and similar expressions, or thatevents or conditions "will" or "may" occur. These statements are subject to various risks.  Although PampaMetals believes the expectations expressed in such forward-lookingstatements are based on reasonable assumptions, such statements arenot guaranteeing of future performance, and actual results may differmaterially from those in forward-looking statements.

data.title: Miner Vale's Q2 net profit falls 24% but outdoes analyst forecasts
data.description:
Brazilian miner Vale reported on Thursday a 24% decline in its second-quarter net profit from a year earlier to $2.12 billion, while still outperforming analyst expectations.

Analysts polled by LSEG had expected Vale to post a $1.44 billion net profit for the quarter ended in June.

The miner matched analyst expectations for revenue, posting $8.8 billion.

Revenue declined in Vale's key iron ore segment compared to a year ago, hurt by smaller sales volumes and lower prices, but grew in both copper and nickel.

At the same time, all-in costs decreased from last year in each category - 10% in iron ore, 60% in copper and 30% in nickel - due to efficiency measures and higher output.

Vale also said it spent $200 million less this quarter than a year before, keeping it on track to meet its 2025 guidance of $5.9 billion.

data.title: Visa Is Maintained at Overweight by Barclays
Stanford University to Sell $500M of Taxable Bonds
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Stanford University plans to sell $500 million of taxable bonds that received AAA ratings from each of the major credit rating firms.

Pricing for the Series 2025 B bonds may take place as early as next week, according to a roadshow document posted Thursday on MuniOS. Proceeds are slated to be used for general university purposes.

Stanford's General Use Permit, approved by Santa Clara County in 2000, permitted the school to develop over 2 million square feet of new academic facilities and 4,468 new housing units for students, faculty and staff.

The permit limits new development but not removal, renovation or rehabilitation of existing facilities. The school is using its remaining allotments for renovations at existing sites on its main campus and developing facilities in other jurisdictions, including its Redwood City campus.

According to a roadshow document, the school's financial strengths include a 2.4% increase in net assets to $52.5 billion in fiscal 2024. Operating revenue for the year, reflecting only the university and not Stanford Health Care and Lucile Salter Packard Children's Hospital, was $8.4 billion.

The bonds received the top rating from Fitch Ratings, Moody's and S&P Global Ratings. Fitch said its ratings reflect the school's "exceptional demand profile, strong research platform, robust financial profile, and excellent fundraising track record of supporting strategic and capital investment needs."

Stanford said it continues to monitor developments involving the federal government and noted that actions at the federal level could reduce support for the university.

Fitch said its stable outlook "reflects ample financial flexibility against potential revenue pressure in fiscal 2026 and beyond, including ongoing developments involving the federal government."

Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are the lead underwriters.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Fortune Brands Beats on 2Q Results, Expects to Offset Tariff Costs
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Fortune Brands Innovation second-quarter results topped estimates and company said it is on track to offset tariff costs.

The home and security products maker on Thursday posted a profit of $100.3 million, or 83 cents a share, in the second quarter, compared with $133.9 million, or $1.06 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $1, ahead of the 96 cents forecast by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue fell 3% to $1.20 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $1.19 billion.

The company is positioned to fully offset costs from tariffs this year, Chief Executive Nicholas Fink said. Sales are expected to be flat to down 2% in the full year.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Materials Down on Mixed Earnings, as International Paper Slides — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell sharply after mixed earnings.

Shares of International Paper plunged after unexpectedly high costs overshadowed an increase in revenue, causing a precipitous drop in net income.

Canadian uranium producer Cameco lost ground after it posted earnings shy of some investors' expectations and named a new chief operating officer and chief financial officer. Uranium prices and the company's sales to the U.S. could be affected by tariffs.

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said bilateral talks with Washington to resolve the tariff row might not lead to a deal before the Aug. 1 deadline.

Albemarle is tightening its operations and cutting costs in an effort to better navigate what Finance Chief Neal Sheorey called a volatile and dynamic lithium market. Lithium prices have fluctuated, rising sharply in 2022 and 2023 in anticipation of rising electric-vehicle demand, and subsequently crashing following the slowdown in EV sales.

Vulcan Materials ticked up after the provider of building materials such as construction aggregates logged quarterly revenue growth.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Health Care Down as Cigna Weighs — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Cigna shares plunged after the health insurer echoed warnings from rivals such as UnitedHealth Group, warning that increased medical costs were crimping earnings.

"Our planning assumption within Cigna Healthcare for 2025 is that we will continue to experience elevated medical costs," said Chief Operating Officer Brian Evanko.

Investors have fled the health-insurance niche this year, concerned that the business model no longer works in an aging population seeking ever-more frequent and expensive medical care.

Pharmacy chain CVS Health reported earnings that beat Wall Street's expectations and upgraded its projection for 2025, as its health-insurance business showed signs of recovery.

Moderna plans to cut 10% of its workforce, a humbling moment for the company that became the best-known biotech in the world during the Covid-19 pandemic.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Ameren Corp reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Ameren Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.01​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 97 cents. The mean expectation of eleven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 98 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 93 cents to $1.05 per share.

  • Revenue rose 31.2% to $2.22 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.78 billion.

  • Ameren Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.01​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $275 million.

  • Ameren Corp shares had risen by 4.0% this quarter and gained 12.0% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 4.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, six analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," 6 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the multiline utilities peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ameren Corp is $104.00, about 2.8% above its last closing price of $101.13

data.title: UMB Financial Is Maintained at Overweight by Barclays
Annuity Sales Set Another Record. $100 Billion a Quarter Is the 'New Normal.' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kenneth Corbin

Annuity sales set another record in the second quarter, topping $116 billion. It's the seventh consecutive quarter that sales exceeded $100 billion, according to the trade group Limra. Stabilizing interest rates could chill sales in the second half of the year, but Limra nonetheless expects a brisk third and fourth quarter.

"[W]e see a slight softening in the market, which could result in a contraction in the second half of the year," says Bryan Hodgens, senior vice president and head of Limra research. "Even accounting for the potential dip in sales in the second half of the year, annuity sales will likely surpass $400 billion in 2025."

By category, fixed-rate deferred, or FRD, annuities accounted for the largest share of sales at $44.2 billion in the second quarter, up 9% from the same period in 2024. For these kinds of annuities, insurers set an initial interest rate for a certain period, after which they sometimes offer a new rate, though that is typically bounded by a minimum. Once the product reaches the withdrawal stage, insurers make regular payments for a set period of time or for a lifetime.

Despite the strong quarterly showing, that category of annuities is showing some softening due to interest rate and market performance, according to Keith Golembiewski, assistant vice president and director of Limra annuity research.

"Although FRD sales have rebounded from the sluggish start in the first quarter, monthly data suggest sales are sliding as interest rates stabilize and likely investors have already secured FRD contracts," he says.

Surging equity markets have also weighed on FRD sales, according to Golembiewski, though he notes that those types of annuities generally have been offering better rates than Certificates of Deposit, "making them the most attractive, short-term solutions for risk-averse investors."

Registered index linked annuities, or RILAs, which offer tax-deferred growth with returns correlated with the performance of a stock index, set a new record in the second quarter with sales of $19.6 billion, up 20% from the year-earlier period. First-half RILA sales in 2025 also jumped 20% from last year.

"Limra expects the growth trajectory for RILA [sales] will continue for the foreseeable future, especially as more broker-dealers add RILAs into their product mix," Golembiewski says.

Sales of fixed-index annuities or FIAs, which are structured like RILAs but offer more downside risk protection so investors won't lose money if the tracking index slumps, checked in at $31.4 billion in the second quarter, even with the year-ago period. Golembiewski attributes some of that to "cannibalization" from FRD sales, but notes that FIAs' "value proposition remains very strong as clients and financial professionals look for guaranteed income and protected growth."

Hodgens sees the nearly two years' worth of quarterly annuity sales topping $100 billion as the product of the insurance industry's work to promote the value of guaranteed income to investors and the financial advisors they work with.

"This 'new normal' in the U.S. annuity market is partially because of industry efforts to educate consumers and advisors about the value of including a guaranteed lifetime income stream into a retirement plan," he says. "But the work needs to continue — today, just one in five preretirees own an annuity, and nearly half say they won't have enough guaranteed income to cover basic living expenses."

Corrections and amplifications: This story was updated to reflect that annuity sales exceeded $100 billion in the quarter. An earlier version of the story reported an incorrect figure.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
